stimulus: According to a well-documented pattern in linguistic typology, languages whose verbs come at the very end of the clause tend strongly toward postpositions, in which grammatical markers follow the noun they govern. ______blank languages whose verbs sit between the subject and object generally favor prepositions, in which those same grammatical markers appear in front of the noun.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. "Conversely" logically signals that the information in this sentence—that languages with verbs between subject and object favor prepositions—contrasts with the previous information about verb-final languages favoring postpositions.

Choice A is incorrect because "for instance" illogically signals that the second case exemplifies the first, when the two cases instead present opposed patterns. Choice B is incorrect because "in other words" illogically signals a restatement of the previous information, when the second sentence actually presents the opposite pattern. Choice C is incorrect because "subsequently" illogically signals a chronological sequence, when the two clauses present a contrast in linguistic patterns rather than a temporal order.
